✨ AI Study Tutor

Powered by Google Gemini — uses your course notes directly from GitHub as its knowledge base. Your API key is stored only in your browser.

🔑 How to get your free Gemini API key
1
Go to aistudio.google.com/apikey and sign in with your free Google account.
2
Click "Create API Key" — choose Create new project when prompted. Copy the key that appears.
3
Paste it below and click Confirm Key. It is saved only in your browser's localStorage — it never leaves your device.
4
Free tier limits: 15 requests/min and 1 million tokens/day. If you hit a rate limit error, you can create a new project (not just a new key in the same project — a new project gets a fresh quota). Go back to AI Studio → select "Create new project" → create a new key there.
Enter your key above.
⚠️ Hit a rate limit? You must create a new project in AI Studio — not just a new API key. Keys in the same project share the same quota. Go to aistudio.google.com/apikey, click the project selector at the top, then New project.
What would you like to do?
📝
Topic Review
Tell the AI what you know about a topic. It checks your answer against the course notes and tells you what's right, what's missing, and what's wrong.
🏥
Practical Scenarios
Get a clinical scenario (e.g. "Perform thoracocentesis on a dyspnoeic dog"). Describe your procedure and the AI grades your technique.
🎯
Generate Questions
The AI generates new exam-style questions from the course notes for any topic — great for drilling beyond the built-in question bank.
📋
Topic Summary
Ask for a concise summary of any topic — key facts, normal values, clinical pearls — drawn directly from your course notes.
📝 Topic Review
1. Select a topic
2. Write everything you know about this topic
Don't hold back — the more you write, the more detailed the feedback. Include normal values, procedures, species differences, clinical signs — anything relevant.
No topic selected
🏥 Practical Scenarios
1. Select a topic for the scenario
📋 Clinical Scenario
Describe your procedure step by step

🔬 Special Pathology

Select a topic below to begin studying. Each topic contains subtopic notes, questions, practicals, and past exam questions.

📚 Topics
Available
🧠
Neuropathology

CNS cells, injury responses, defences, infections, malformations, degenerative diseases, tumours, and PNS disorders.

13 Subtopics27 Pages
Available
🦋
Endocrine System

General principles, pituitary, adrenal, thyroid, parathyroid, pancreatic islets, chemoreceptor organs, pineal gland, and endocrine adipose tissue.

15 Subtopics16 Pages
Available
🫧
Lymphatic System

Thymus, spleen, lymph node diseases, MALT, infectious diseases (Leishmaniasis, Histoplasmosis), and lymphoma across species.

7 Subtopics
Coming Soon
🫁
Pathology of Respiratory System

Upper and lower airway diseases, pneumonias, pleural diseases, neoplasia.

Coming Soon
Available
🫀
Digestive System

Oral cavity, oesophagus, stomach, intestines, liver, pancreas.

14 Subtopics
Available
🟤
Hepatobiliary System

Liver structure, response to injury, failure, circulatory and metabolic disease, infections, toxins, neoplasia, biliary tract, and exocrine pancreas.

15 Subtopics
Available
🩹
Pathology of the Skin

Epidermal, dermal, pigmentary and adnexal responses to injury, congenital disorders, physical/chemical injury, infections, immunologic disease, and tumours.

14 Subtopics
Available
♂️
Male Reproductive System

Defence mechanisms, disorders of sexual development, scrotum, testis, epididymis, spermatic cord, accessory glands, penis and prepuce, and related neoplasia.

14 Subtopics
Available
♀️
Female Reproductive System

Portals of entry and defences, congenital disorders, vagina, cervix, uterus, pregnancy pathology, oviduct, ovaries, and mammary gland.

14 Subtopics
Available
🦴
Bone Marrow & Blood Cells

Haematopoiesis regulation, bone marrow reactions to injury, cytopenias, cytoses, congenital disorders, IMHA, infectious diseases, and haematopoietic neoplasia.

10 Subtopics
Available
❤️
Cardiovascular System

Heart failure, congenital anomalies, pericardial and valvular diseases, myocardial pathology, cardiomyopathies, cardiac neoplasia, vascular diseases, and lymphatic disorders.

10 Subtopics
Available
🦷
Bones & Joints

Bone structure, growth and development disorders, osteochondrosis, metabolic bone diseases, osteomyelitis, hip dysplasia, arthritis, and degenerative joint disease.

8 Subtopics
Available
🫘
Urinary System

Renal failure syndromes, developmental anomalies, glomerular diseases, tubular necrosis, infectious nephritis, hydronephrosis, renal neoplasia, and lower urinary tract disorders.

10 Subtopics
Available
👁️
Pathology of the Eye

Ocular structure, globe malformations, glaucoma, corneal, conjunctival, uveal and retinal diseases, and ocular neoplasia.

7 Subtopics
Available
💪
Skeletal Muscles

Myofiber alterations, degenerative myopathies, bacterial and clostridial myositis, parasitic and immune-mediated myositis, congenital myopathies, and endocrine disturbances.

6 Subtopics
